aced the `protected_call_raw` `todo!()` stub.

**What it does now
attern. Invokes the existing `do_::pcall` helper with a closure `|s| s.call_no_yield(func, n
results)`, using `func` as the `old_top` and `errfunc.0 as isize` as the error-function off
set. On `LuaStatus::Ok`, returns `Ok(())`. On `ErrRun` / `ErrSyntax`, retrieves the
error value that `do_::pcall`'s `set_error_obj` placed at `func`, resets `top` to `func`, and wraps the
value into the matching `LuaError` variant (Runtime/Syntax). Other statuses (`ErrMem`, `ErrEr
r`, etc.) reset top and use `LuaError::with_status`. This matches the calling contract used by `state.prot

(e)` means "caller pushes `e.into_value()` onto the stack themselves."

s reachable next:** Calling `call_no_yield` will exercise `do_::callnoyield`, which itself dispatches into

op iteration.

content.text:
Build is green.

## Report

**Modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1260` — replaced the `protected_call_raw` `todo!()` stub.

**What it does now:** Mirrors C `lua_pcallk`'s `f_call` + `luaD_pcall` pattern. Invokes the existing `do_::pcall` helper with a closure `|s| s.call_no_yield(func, nresults)`, using `func` as the `old_top` and `errfunc.0 as isize` as the error-function offset. On `LuaStatus::Ok`, returns `Ok(())`. On `ErrRun` / `ErrSyntax`, retrieves the error value that `do_::pcall`'s `set_error_obj` placed at `func`, resets `top` to `func`, and wraps the value into the matching `LuaError` variant (Runtime/Syntax). Other statuses (`ErrMem`, `ErrErr`, etc.) reset top and use `LuaError::with_status`. This matches the calling contract used by `state.protected_call(...)` in stdlib base.rs `pcall_fn`, where `Err(e)` means "caller pushes `e.into_value()` onto the stack themselves."

**Latent todo!()s reachable next:** Calling `call_no_yield` will exercise `do_::callnoyield`, which itself dispatches into `precall` / `do_call` / `poscall` — those are still `todo!()` stubs at lines 1264, 1265, 1269, 1270, 1272 and will surface on the next loop iteration.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).

## Report

**Modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1260` — replaced the `protected_call_raw` `todo!()` stub.

**What it does now:** Mirrors C `lua_pcallk`'s `f_call` + `luaD_pcall` pattern. Invokes the existing `do_::pcall` helper with a closure `|s| s.call_no_yield(func, nresults)`, using `func` as the `old_top` and `errfunc.0 as isize` as the error-function offset. On `LuaStatus::Ok`, returns `Ok(())`. On `ErrRun` / `ErrSyntax`, retrieves the error value that `do_::pcall`'s `set_error_obj` placed at `func`, resets `top` to `func`, and wraps the value into the matching `LuaError` variant (Runtime/Syntax). Other statuses (`ErrMem`, `ErrErr`, etc.) reset top and use `LuaError::with_status`. This matches the calling contract used by `state.protected_call(...)` in stdlib base.rs `pcall_fn`, where `Err(e)` means "caller pushes `e.into_value()` onto the stack themselves."

**Latent todo!()s reachable next:** Calling `call_no_yield` will exercise `do_::callnoyield`, which itself dispatches into `precall` / `do_call` / `poscall` — those are still `todo!()` stubs at lines 1264, 1265, 1269, 1270, 1272 and will surface on the next loop iteration.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors).
